review.reason: MCP server and connector workflows are high-value but require explicit network, filesystem, credential, and external-system mutation boundary review.
review.action: Review as source-profile candidate and identify whether any procedure should remain reference-only, require risk audit, or enter normalization queue.
review.outcome: Reviewed as reference-only source profile. Created risk item for AWS/cloud MCP boundary before any normalization or install endorsement.

review.reason: Benchmark and evaluation harnesses can improve validation gates for skills and workflows without copying benchmark content.
review.action: Review license, evaluation shape, failure taxonomy, and whether it can inform acceptance criteria for skill usefulness validation.

risk.reason: Source is useful for agent-supply-chain review, but its documented MCP scanning mode may execute commands from MCP configs and can involve tokens, network/API analysis, and local discovery across agent runtimes.
risk.action: Decide whether to distill only a safe review checklist into existing security doctrine, preserving no-clone, no-execute, no-token, no-network-default scheduler boundaries.
risk.outcome: Closed as reference-only. No scanner execution, token handling, MCP server startup, network/API submission, background monitoring, or package recommendation is allowed in the scheduler. No normalization item was created because the existing MCP external tool security skill already covers the core boundary.

risk.reason: Source is a high-value MCP catalogue, but individual servers can involve package-runner installation, AWS credentials, network access, local command execution, and cloud infrastructure mutation.
risk.action: Decide whether to distill only a safe MCP catalogue/cloud-tool boundary checklist into existing security doctrine, preserving no-clone, no-execute, no-credential, no-cloud-mutation scheduler boundaries.
risk.outcome: Closed as reference-only. No AWS MCP server startup, package-runner execution, editor MCP configuration change, AWS credential handling, remote managed MCP invocation, or cloud-resource mutation is allowed in the scheduler. No normalization item was created because the existing MCP external tool security review skill already covers the core boundary.
